'''二十五柱神社'''(にじゅうごはしらじんじゃ)は、[[三重県]][[松阪市]]柿木原町に鎮座する神社。
[[明治]]41年、宇気比神社に、二十四の神社が合祀されて、二十五柱神社が祀られた([[式内社]]も含む)。
== 祭神 == 祭神は、[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神|伊吹戸主神]]・[[天鈿女命]]・庭高津日神・[[大山咋神]]・[[木花佐久夜毘売命]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[月読命|月弓之命]]・[[大山祇命]]・[[事代主神]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売命]]・[[誉田別命]]・[[少彦名神]]・[[木俣神]]・[[埴山比売神|埴安比売神]]・[[大国主神]]・[[天忍穂耳命]]・[[熊野久須毘命]]・[[天津彦根命]]・[[活津彦根命]]・[[天穂日命|天穂日之命]]・[[多岐都比売命|多岐津比売命]]・[[市杵島比売命]]・[[奥津島比売命|奥津島命]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>。

== 祭神 == 祭神は、[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神|伊吹戸主神]]・[[天鈿女命]]・庭高津日神・[[大山咋神]]・[[木花佐久夜毘売命]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[月読命|月弓之命]]・[[大山祇命]]・[[事代主神]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売命]]・[[誉田別命]]・[[少彦名神]]・[[木俣神]]・[[埴山比売神|埴安比売神]]・[[大国主神]]・[[天忍穂耳命]]・[[熊野久須毘命]]・[[天津彦根命]]・[[活津彦根命]]・[[天穂日命|天穂日之命]]・[[多岐都比売命|多岐津比売命]]・[[市杵島比売命]]・[[奥津島比売命|奥津島命]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>。
== 例祭 == ９月15日。
月次祭が毎月、第２日曜日。
== アクセス == *黒部東コミュニティバス『東黒部市民センター』バス停から徒歩２分(平日のみ) *黒部東コミュニティバス『出間』バス停から徒歩７分(平日のみ) *黒部東コミュニティバス『陽光寺横駐車場』バス停から徒歩８分(平日のみ) *近鉄山田線『櫛田駅』から徒歩55分

'''二十五柱神社'''(にじゅうごはしらじんじゃ)は、[[三重県]][[松阪市]]柿木原町に[[鎮座]]する神社。
「にじゅうごばしら」と呼ばれることもあるが、正式には、「にじゅうごはしら」<ref>神主様宛確認</ref>。
[[明治]]41年、宇気比神社([[東黒部村]]柿木原)に、浜神社(同村東黒部)・宇気比神社(同村南出)・高之宮神社(同村大垣内)・牛庭神社(同村牛草)・林神社(同村出間)・服部麻刀方神社(同村垣内田)・須麻漏売神社(同村垣内田)・山神社(同村東黒部)・八幡宮(同村大垣内)・服部伊刀麻神社(同村出間)・須賀神社(同村東黒部)・事代主神社(同村柿木原)・流田上神社(同村神守)等、二十四の神社が[[合祀]]されて([[式内社]]も含む)、二十五柱神社が祀られた。
== 祭神 == 祭神は、[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神|伊吹戸主神]]・[[天鈿女命]]・庭高津日神・[[大山咋神]]・[[木花佐久夜毘売命]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[月読命|月弓之命]]・[[大山祇命]]・[[事代主神]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売命]]・[[誉田別命]]・[[少彦名神]]・[[木俣神]]・[[埴山比売神|埴安比売神]]・[[大国主神]]・[[天忍穂耳命]]・[[熊野久須毘命]]・[[天津彦根命]]・[[活津彦根命]]・[[天穂日命|天穂日之命]]・[[多岐都比売命|多岐津比売命]]・[[市杵島比売命]]・[[奥津島比売命|奥津島命]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>の24座。
== 例祭 == ９月15日。
月次祭が毎月、第２日曜日。

「にじゅうごばしら」と呼ばれることもあるが、正式には、「にじゅうごはしら」<ref>神主様宛確認</ref>。
[[明治]]41年12月、宇気比神社([[東黒部村]]柿木原)に、浜神社(同村東黒部)・宇気比神社(同村南出)・高之宮神社(同村大垣内)・◎牛庭神社(同村牛草)・◎林神社(同村出間)・◎服部麻刀方神社(同村垣内田)・◎須麻漏売神社(同村垣内田)・山神社(同村東黒部)・八幡宮(同村大垣内)・◎服部伊刀麻神社(同村出間)・須賀神社(同村東黒部)・事代主神社(同村柿木原)・◎流田上神社(同村神守)・◎国乃御神社(同村土古路)・◎宇気比神社(同村乙部)等、二十四の神社が[[合祀]]されて([[式内社]]も含む)、二十五柱神社が祀られ<ref>松阪市史 第3巻参照</ref>、神饌幣帛料供進指定社に変更せらる。(◎は、式内小社。◎宇気比神社は、明治23年式内小社の流田神社を合祀。)
== 祭神 == 祭神は、[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神|伊吹戸主神]]・[[天鈿女命]]・庭高津日神・[[大山咋神]]・[[木花佐久夜毘売命]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[月読命|月弓之命]]・[[大山祇命]]・[[事代主神]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売命]]・[[誉田別命]]・[[少彦名神]]・[[木俣神]]・[[埴山比売神|埴安比売神]]・[[大国主神]]・[[天忍穂耳命]]・[[熊野久須毘命]]・[[天津彦根命]]・[[活津彦根命]]・[[天穂日命|天穂日之命]]・[[多岐都比売命|多岐津比売命]]・[[市杵島比売命]]・[[奥津島比売命|奥津島命]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>の24座。
== 例祭 == ９月15日。
月次祭が毎月、第２日曜日。
== アクセス == *黒部東コミュニティバス『東黒部市民センター』バス停から徒歩２分(平日のみ) *黒部東コミュニティバス『出間』バス停から徒歩７分(平日のみ) *黒部東コミュニティバス『陽光寺横駐車場』バス停から徒歩８分(平日のみ) *近鉄山田線『櫛田駅』から徒歩55分

{{神社 | 名称 = 二十五柱神社 | 画像 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|35|23|N|136|35|59|E}}}} | 所在地 = 三重県松阪市柿木原52 | 緯度度 = 34| 緯度分 = 35| 緯度秒 = 23 | 経度度 = 136| 経度分 = 35| 経度秒 = 59 | 祭神 = [[速佐須良比売神]]、他23座 | 神体 = | 社格 = [[延喜式内社]]と合祀（旧社格：[[村社]]） | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = ９月15日 | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Mie }}
'''二十五柱神社'''(にじゅうごはしらじんじゃ)は、[[三重県]][[松阪市]]柿木原町に[[鎮座]]する神社。
「にじゅうごばしら」と呼ばれることもあるが、正式には、「にじゅうごはしら」<ref>神主様宛確認</ref>。
[[明治]]41年12月、◎宇気比神社([[東黒部村]]柿木原)に、浜神社(同村東黒部)・宇気比神社(同村南出)・高之宮神社(同村大垣内)・◎牛庭神社(同村牛草)・◎林神社(同村出間)旧[[郷社]]・◎服部麻刀方神社(同村垣内田)・◎須麻漏売神社(同村垣内田)・山神社(同村東黒部)・八幡宮(同村大垣内)・◎服部伊刀麻神社(同村出間)・須賀神社(同村東黒部)・事代主神社(同村柿木原)・◎流田上神社(同村神守)・◎国乃御神社(同村土古路)・宇気比神社(同村乙部)等、二十四の神社が[[合祀]]されて([[式内社]]も含む<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/59 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-28}}</ref>)、二十五柱神社が祀られ<ref>松阪市史 第3巻参照</ref>、神饌幣帛料供進指定社に変更された。(◎は、式内小社。◎宇気比神社は、明治23年式内小社の流田神社を合祀のため。)
== 祭神 == 祭神は、[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神|伊吹戸主神]]・[[天鈿女命]]・庭高津日神・[[大山咋神]]・[[木花佐久夜毘売命]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[月読命|月弓之命]]・[[大山祇命]]・[[事代主神]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売命]]・[[誉田別命]]・[[少彦名神]]・[[木俣神]]・[[埴山比売神|埴安比売神]]・[[大国主神]]・[[天忍穂耳命]]・[[熊野久須毘命]]・[[天津彦根命]]・[[活津彦根命]]・[[天穂日命|天穂日之命]]・[[多岐都比売命|多岐津比売命]]・[[市杵島比売命]]・[[奥津島比売命|奥津島命]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>の24座。
== 例祭 == *９月15日
*月次祭が毎月、第２日曜日
== アクセス == *黒部東コミュニティバス『東黒部市民センター』バス停から徒歩２分(平日のみ) *黒部東コミュニティバス『出間』バス停から徒歩７分(平日のみ) *黒部東コミュニティバス『陽光寺横駐車場』バス停から徒歩８分(平日のみ) *近鉄山田線『櫛田駅』から徒歩55分

'''二十五柱神社'''(にじゅうごはしらじんじゃ)は、[[三重県]][[松阪市]]柿木原町に[[鎮座]]する神社。
「にじゅうごばしら」と呼ばれることもあるが、正式には、「にじゅうごはしら」<ref>神主様宛確認</ref>。
[[明治]]41年12月、◎宇気比神社([[東黒部村]]柿木原)に、浜神社(同村東黒部)・宇気比神社(同村南出)・高之宮神社(同村大垣内)・◎牛庭神社(同村牛草)・林神社(同村出間)・◎服部麻刀方神社(同村垣内田)・◎須麻漏売神社(同村垣内田)・山神社(同村東黒部)・八幡宮(同村大垣内)・◎服部伊刀麻神社(同村出間)・須賀神社(同村東黒部)・事代主神社(同村柿木原)・◎流田上神社(同村神守)・◎国乃御神社(同村土古路)・宇気比神社(同村乙部)等、二十四の神社が[[合祀]]されて([[式内社]]も含む<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/59 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-28}}</ref>)、二十五柱神社が祀られ<ref>松阪市史 第3巻参照</ref>、神饌幣帛料供進指定社に変更された。(◎は、式内小社。◎宇気比神社は、明治23年式内小社の流田神社を合祀したため。)
なお、宇気比神社(東黒部村乙部)は、◎火地神社を合祀したという、論社となっている。
== 祭神 == 祭神は、[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神|伊吹戸主神]]・[[天鈿女命]]・庭高津日神・[[大山咋神]]・[[木花佐久夜毘売命]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[月読命|月弓之命]]・[[大山祇命]]・[[事代主神]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売命]]・[[誉田別命]]・[[少彦名神]]・[[木俣神]]・[[埴山比売神|埴安比売神]]・[[大国主神]]・[[天忍穂耳命]]・[[熊野久須毘命]]・[[天津彦根命]]・[[活津彦根命]]・[[天穂日命|天穂日之命]]・[[多岐都比売命|多岐津比売命]]・[[市杵島比売命]]・[[奥津島比売命|奥津島命]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>の24座。

== 祭神 == 祭神は、[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神|伊吹戸主神]]・[[天鈿女命]]・庭高津日神・[[大山咋神]]・[[木花佐久夜毘売命]]・[[須佐之男命]]・[[月読命|月弓之命]]・[[大山祇命]]・[[事代主神]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売命]]・[[誉田別命]]・[[少彦名神]]・[[木俣神]]・[[埴山比売神|埴安比売神]]・[[大国主神]]・[[天忍穂耳命]]・[[熊野久須毘命]]・[[天津彦根命]]・[[活津彦根命]]・[[天穂日命|天穂日之命]]・[[多岐都比売命|多岐津比売命]]・[[市杵島比売命]]・[[奥津島比売命|奥津島命]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>の24座。

== アクセス == *黒部東コミュニティバス『東黒部市民センター』バス停から徒歩２分(平日のみ) *黒部東コミュニティバス『出間』バス停から徒歩７分(平日のみ) *黒部東コミュニティバス『陽光寺横駐車場』バス停から徒歩８分(平日のみ) *近鉄山田線『櫛田駅』から徒歩55分

revision.text:
'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Hondawake no Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}

(◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Hondawake no Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Hondawake no Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}

(◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

revision.text:
'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />

revision.text:
'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines 24 deities: [[Hayasasurahime no Kami|Hayasasura-hime]], [[Ibukidonushi no Kami|Ibuki-tonushi]], [[Amenouzume no Mikoto|Amenouzume]], Niwatakatsuhiko no Kami, [[Ōyamazumi no Kami]], [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]], [[Ōyamatsumi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Toyouke no Ōmikami]], [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], [[Sukunabikona no Kami]], [[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Haniyasuhime no Kami]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Ama no Oshihomimi no Mikoto]], [[Kumano no Kusuhi no Kami]], [[Amatsuhikone no Kami]], [[Ikutahikone no Kami]], [[Ama no Hohihi no Mikoto]], [[Takitsuhime no Kami]], [[Ichikishimahime no Mikoto]], and [[Okitsushima-hime]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=Eight Million Gods |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" />
== Deities Enshrined ==
The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:
* {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Ibukidonushi|lt_ja=伊吹戸主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命}} * {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神}} * {{ill|Ōyamakuhi|ja|大山咋神}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花佐久夜毘売命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命|lt=Tsukiyumi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=月弓之命}} * {{ill|Ōyamakui|ja|大山祇命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} * {{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|豊受比売|lt=Toyoukehime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=豊受比売命}} * {{ill|Honda Wake|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Kimatakami|ja|木俣神}} * {{ill|Haniyamahime|ja|埴山比売神|lt=Haniyasuhime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=埴安比売神}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * {{ill|Kumano Kusubi|ja|熊野久須毘命}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津彦根命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命|lt=Ame-no-hohi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天穂日之命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=多岐津比売命}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}} * {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命}}

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" /> [[ja:二十五柱神社]]
== Deities Enshrined ==
The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:
* {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Ibukidonushi|lt_ja=伊吹戸主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命}} * {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神}} * {{ill|Ōyamakuhi|ja|大山咋神}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花佐久夜毘売命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命|lt=Tsukiyumi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=月弓之命}} * {{ill|Ōyamakui|ja|大山祇命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} * {{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|豊受比売|lt=Toyoukehime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=豊受比売命}} * {{ill|Honda Wake|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Kimatakami|ja|木俣神}} * {{ill|Haniyamahime|ja|埴山比売神|lt=Haniyasuhime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=埴安比売神}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * {{ill|Kumano Kusubi|ja|熊野久須毘命}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津彦根命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命|lt=Ame-no-hohi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天穂日之命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=多岐津比売命}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}} * {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命}}
<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>

== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

revision.text:
'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.
While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
== Overview == In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>
Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} under {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}).<ref name=":0" /> [[ja:二十五柱神社]]
== Deities Enshrined ==
The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:
* {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Ibukidonushi|lt_ja=伊吹戸主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命}} * {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神}} * {{ill|Ōyamakuhi|ja|大山咋神}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花佐久夜毘売命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命|lt=Tsukiyumi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=月弓之命}} * {{ill|Ōyamakui|ja|大山祇命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} * {{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|豊受比売|lt=Toyoukehime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=豊受比売命}} * {{ill|Honda Wake|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神}} * {{ill|Kimatakami|ja|木俣神}} * {{ill|Haniyamahime|ja|埴山比売神|lt=Haniyasuhime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=埴安比売神}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * {{ill|Kumano Kusubi|ja|熊野久須毘命}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}} * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津彦根命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|天穂日命|lt=Ame-no-hohi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天穂日之命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=多岐津比売命}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}} * {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命}}
<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>
[[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[ja:祭神]]
== Annual Festivals == * September 15th * Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month
== Access == * 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only) * 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only) * 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only) * 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}}

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}

* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}}

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}
* {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

* {{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|豊受比売|lt=Toyoukehime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=豊受比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}

* {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像三女神|1=Three Goddesses of Munakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10948069}}

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}
* {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

* {{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|豊受比売|lt=Toyoukehime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=豊受比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}

* {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
* {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像三女神|1=Three Goddesses of Munakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10948069}}

<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}
* {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Annual Festivals ==
* September 15th 
* Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month

== Access ==
* 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only)
* 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only)
* 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only)
* 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}}

* {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

* {{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|豊受比売|lt=Toyoukehime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=豊受比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}

* {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像三女神|1=Three Goddesses of Munakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10948069}}

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>
