'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}}

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}
* {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakuhi]]
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花佐久夜毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命|lt=Tsukiyumi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=月弓之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}
* {{ill|Ōyamakui|ja|大山祇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamakui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
* {{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|豊受比売|lt=Toyoukehime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=豊受比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}

* {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|lt=Ame-no-hohi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天穂日之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}
* {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}
* {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
* {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像三女神|1=Three Goddesses of Munakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10948069}}

<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}
* {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}

== Annual Festivals ==
* September 15th 
* Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month

== Access ==
* 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only)
* 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only)
* 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only)
* 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}

* {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像三女神|1=Three Goddesses of Munakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10948069}}

<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>

== Annual Festivals ==
* September 15th 
* Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month

== Access ==
* 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only)
* 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only)
* 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only)
* 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>

== Annual Festivals ==
* September 15th 
* Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month

== Access ==
* 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only)
* 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only)
* 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only)
* 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

(◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under [[:en:Taki District, Mie|Taki District]]).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}}

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}
* {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

* {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
* {{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|豊受比売|lt=Toyoukehime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=豊受比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}

* {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
* {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像三女神|1=Three Goddesses of Munakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10948069}}

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二十五柱神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二十五柱神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二十五柱神社]]


'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District|1=Taki District, Mie}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|lt_ja=伊吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}
* {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakuhi|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花佐久夜毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月読命|lt=Tsukiyumi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=月弓之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}
* {{ill|Ōyamakui|ja|大山祇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamakui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
* {{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|豊受比売|lt=Toyoukehime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=豊受比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
* {{ill|Honda Wake|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honda Wake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}
* {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
* {{ill|Kimatakami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimatakami|1=Kimata-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092833}}
* {{ill|Haniyamahime|ja|埴山比売神|lt=Haniyasuhime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=埴安比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9285919}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}

* {{ill|Kumano Kusubi|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ|1=Kumanokusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11298796}}
* {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}
* {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}
* {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|lt=Ame-no-hohi-no-mikoto|lt_ja=天穂日之命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}
* {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}
* {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
* {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10948069}}

<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>

== Annual Festivals ==
* September 15th 
* Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month

== Access ==
* 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only)
* 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only)
* 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only)
* 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District|1=Taki District, Mie}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Annual Festivals ==
* September 15th 
* Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month

== Access ==
* 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only)
* 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only)
* 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only)
* 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二十五柱神社]]


'''Nijūgohashira Shrine''' (二十五柱神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Kakigihara Town, Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Japan.

While commonly pronounced "Nijūgobashira," the formal pronunciation is "Nijūgohashira."<ref>Confirmed with the shrine priest.</ref>

== Overview == 
In December 1908 (Meiji 41), multiple shrines, including ◎Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), Hama Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Minamide), Takamiya Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Ushiba Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ushikusa), Hayashi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), ◎Hattori Matokata Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), ◎Sumarome Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kainota), Yamami Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Hachiman Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Ōgaito), ◎Hattori Itoma Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Dema), Suga Shrine (Higashikurobe Village), Kotoshironushi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kakigihara), ◎Nagaredaue Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Kamimori), ◎Kuninomi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Tokoroji), and Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe), were merged, forming Nijūgohashira Shrine, which was subsequently designated as a site for official offerings. (◎ indicates shrines designated as Shikinaisha or small shrines; ◎Ukehi Shrine merged Nagare Shrine in 1890, recognized as a Shikinaisha.)<ref name=":0">Refer to Volume 3 of Matsusaka City History.</ref>

Ukehi Shrine (Higashikurobe Village, Otobe) merged the Shikinaisha Hinokuchi Shrine, creating a contested site (refer to {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} under {{ill|Taki District, Mie|en|Taki District, Mie|WD=Q1042360|lt=Taki District|1=Taki District, Mie}}).<ref name=":0" />

== Deities Enshrined ==

The enshrined deities (祭神) are the 24 kami listed below:

* {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}}

* {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}
* {{ill|Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja|庭高津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niwatakatsuhino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ōyamakui|ja|大山祇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamakui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
* {{ill|Toyoukehime|ja|豊受比売|lt=Toyoukehime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=豊受比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}

* {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}
* {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
* {{ill|Okitsushima|ja|奥津島比売命|lt=Okitsushima-no-mikoto|lt_ja=奥津島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10948069}}

<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://yaokami.jp/1240266/ |title=八百万の神 |access-date=2024-5-26}}</ref>

== Annual Festivals ==
* September 15th 
* Monthly ceremonies on the second Sunday of each month

== Access ==
* 2 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Higashikurobe Civic Center" stop (weekdays only)
* 7 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Dema" stop (weekdays only)
* 8 minutes on foot from the Higashikurobe Community Bus "Yokoji Side Parking Lot" stop (weekdays only)
* 55 minutes on foot from Kintetsu Yamada Line "Kushida Station"
